originalTitle: Grieving Iranian mother tells UN about children before school attack
title: A Brave Mum's Big Wish for Safe Schools!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you a very special story from our amazing continent of Africa and beyond! Today, we are going to talk about a very brave mum and a very important message.

Imagine a mum who loves her children very, very much. She wants them to be happy and safe every single day. Well, there's a mum far away, in a country called Iran, who has a big message for everyone.

She spoke up in a very important place, a place where big grown-ups from all over the world meet. She spoke about her children and how much she wants all children everywhere to be safe, especially at school. Isn't that a wonderful thing to wish for?

What is the Big Meeting About?

Did you know there's a huge meeting place called the United Nations? It's like a very, very big classroom for grown-ups!

Important people from nearly every country in the world go there. They talk about how to make the world a better, safer, and fairer place for everyone. They especially care about children, just like you!

They talk about things like making sure every child has food to eat, a warm bed to sleep in, and a school to go to. They also talk about making sure everyone is kind to each other and that no one gets hurt. It’s a very important job they do!

This brave mum went to one of these special meetings. She went to a part of the United Nations that focuses on 'Human Rights.' That just means making sure every person is treated well and has what they need to live a happy life, including being safe.

A Mother's Brave Story

This mum felt very, very sad. She missed her children so much. Something very sad happened where her children went to school, and the school was not safe that day.

She wanted to share her feelings and her story with all the important grown-ups at the big meeting. She wanted them to hear her heart's wish.

She told them about the last time she saw her children. She remembered their smiles, their hugs, and their happy faces as they went off to learn and play. It was a very hard thing for her to do, to talk about her sadness, but she was so brave.

Why did she do it? She did it because she loves all children. She wants to make sure that no other mum ever has to feel this kind of sadness. She wants all schools, everywhere in the world, to be super-duper safe places for kids.

Did you know that every child has a right to be safe? It's true! Grown-ups have a very important job to make sure children are protected, no matter where they are.

Why Schools are So Special

Schools are amazing places, aren't they? They are where you learn new things, like reading exciting books, counting big numbers, and drawing colourful pictures.

But schools are more than just places to learn. They are also places to make new friends, play fun games, and sing happy songs. Schools are like a second home, full of laughter and discovery.

That's why it's so important that schools are always safe. When you go to school, you should feel happy and secure, knowing that nothing bad will happen. You should be able to focus on learning and having fun, without any worries.

Imagine going to school and knowing you are completely safe. That's the dream of this brave mum, and it's a dream that important grown-ups at the United Nations want to make true for every child.

Did you know that many heroes around the world work hard every day to keep children safe in schools? They are like superheroes of safety!

Wishes for All Children

This mum's message was not just for the grown-ups at the big meeting. It was a message for all of us.

It was a message that reminds us that children are precious. Every child deserves to grow up in peace, to learn, to play, and to feel loved and safe.

Here in Africa, we also want our schools to be the safest places ever! We want every child to walk to school with a smile and come home with even more smiles, full of new knowledge and happy memories.

What can you do? You can be kind to your friends. You can listen to your teachers and grown-ups who keep you safe. And you can always tell a grown-up if something makes you feel worried or unsafe.

This brave mum used her voice to make a change. And guess what? Your voice is important too! When you talk about what's important, grown-ups listen.

So, let's all wish for safe schools, safe homes, and safe communities for every child, everywhere. Let's make sure that every child can learn and play without worry, just like this brave mum wishes.

Did you know that when people speak up for what is right, even big changes can happen? One voice can start a whole world of goodness!

Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends. Until next time, stay safe and keep shining bright!
summary: A brave mother from Iran spoke at a big world meeting to share her sadness about her children and to ask all grown-ups to make sure every school is a very safe place for children everywhere.
whyMatters: This story matters because it reminds all grown-ups how important it is to keep children safe, especially at school, so every child can learn and play without any worries.
